title: Sugar Bytes Looperator Plugin Show & Reveal - Overview
description:
http://bit.ly/1R3cJLU

Plugin Boutique presents a Sugar Bytes Looperator plugin overview.

Looperator is a state-of-the-art loop design powerhouse that no electronic musician should be without, capable of intense technical performances and also casual instant DJ-like fun.
 
For more information visit here: http://bit.ly/1R3cJLU
transcript: [Music] in this movie I'm going to show you the huge benefits of sugar btes looperator which is slapped all over this anak rice remix I've done of Bey man's Mountain Side as you can see I've got multiple instances of the plugin on pretty much every track being used to add brief accents or tweaks here and there or massively chop up and transform certain sections the remix basically came together in a single evening because of lat's huge range of features and ease of use which enables you to create really complex sounding edits like this section here in no time at all in case you don't know what lator does it's a multix matrix on a similar tip to their other software only allowing even more customization and also slicing and looping facilities which are all done with a few clicks of the mouse anyone who's familiar with effect Trix or egoist won't be a stranger to the grid here whereby you've got a column of headings followed by a row of 16 steps which can have minim crotchet or Quaver timings so with crotchet or quarter note here on this guitar track every step is a beat and so the whole Matrix adds up to four bars in total which is the length of the original guitar phrase I'm processing here's what it sounds like with and without looperator so you can hear the combined result of the four different effects being applied which are pitched stutters vinyl scratches tape stops and filter sweeps just to show you some examples of different things we can do to the guitar Loop I'll add another instance to the track and turn off the other one temporarily and I'll initialize everything so the plug's not doing anything now the unprocessed guitar loop as it is has a different cord every bar creating a fair bit of harmonic movement one thing we could do is use the slicing section to rearrange the harmony for example we might want to change the order of the chords at the moment we've got a rising then falling pattern happening from bar to bar but what we could do is swap around the second and fourth bars which will make the Harmony rise from one bar to the next and then fall right at the end you can see at the top the plugin analyzes the audio as it plays so we got the whole guitar Loop here and numbered slices for each bit so to shift the last bar to the second one I can just click on the slice steps here and then choose the slices I want to play so I'll ENT to 13 to 16 in the second bar and then 5 to8 in the last one or what I could do is have the first and second bars looping to simplify the harmony so I'll erase these steps which you can do either by clicking on a step and then selecting X or by clicking on a blank step and then dragging across everything which pastes that step and so deletes everything and now I'll choose 1 to four in the second bar and then 9 to 12 in the last one which makes those bars repeat maybe erase the last slice of those bars so we get a brief Harmony shift at the end of each two bar section and the looping line below then allows you to Loop slices so can create even faster repeats on each slice to make different stutter effects so we could have the last bead of the bar there looping a couple of times or four times or an even faster amount as well as a few different presets that have reverse bits in and if you're not finding what you want in the list here you can create your own custom settings with four different presets on offer all of which allow you to set values for number of repeats panning pitch and amplitude you can even have each value modulated by a curve of some kind but we'll get on to that in a sec first up I thought I'd show you how this looping and slicing allows you to really quickly create a classic Rising buildup you might find in a breakdown so we've now got our four bar guitar part again and what I want this time is to have the first bar looping in smaller amounts as it progresses so I'm going to have slices 1 to four in the second bar so it repeats and then have the first two slices repeating a couple of times in the third bar and then just the First Slice repeating on its own in the last bar and what I'll do now is use Looper to create some faster stutters on the last two slices with two and then four repeats so we get it increasing in Pace even more at the end so now our Loops rearranged we could apply some effects like maybe a filter sweeper some kind again in the filter section we can just click on any step and then choose a filter pattern which allows you to create whatever low or high pass or forant filter sweep you like so we could have some low pass filter modulation throughout the whole phrase if we wanted to or we could create a longer effect instead like maybe have the filter cut off rising up gradually across the whole section which we could do by choosing an upwards low paath slope and then on the next step choosing TI which has the effect of extending that slope across two steps or if we set the other steps to tie as well then it goes across the whole lot but what I might do here is customize the slope myself rather than using the preset which is rising up a bit too quickly so I'll select one of the user presets and then click on the settings button and U1 then keep it on regular filter and have an exponential slope on a low pass then the start and end cuto values can be selected using the two sliders on either side of the graph here and I can set the resonance to and notice that it's possible to have the left and right channels linked or set independently if you want to create stereo effects so there's loads of scope here and finally I'm just going to add a side chaining style effect on the envelope row above which has a load of different level modulation presets for creating pumping tremolo and other Dynamic shaping and I might also make it a bit subtler by bringing down the amount with the slider at the end so that's the basic effect sequence done and you can see how quick it is to set up we in live I would have needed to add all the different effects and set up automation and so on and this is without even going into the two lower sections where you can set up different delays Distortion Reverb pitching effects and loads more and all the sections have randomizer buttons too so if you're not going for a specific effect and just want to mash it up and add some craziness then you can have a play with those and use the undo button at the top to go back if you need to so I massively recommend looperator to any producers wanting to speed up their workflow and have loads more fun with effect processing to find out more about it check the plug-in Boutique website and to listen to the remix in the movie head to the anaki rice SoundCloud page thanks for watching and see you next time m
